description: Trigger accuracy queries for the plan-it skill. Tests that plan-it fires for concrete planning, task breakdown, sequencing, and task-file requests, and stays silent for implementation, review, exploratory, or single-fix requests.

should_trigger.query: plan the work to add project invitations and create task files
should_trigger.note: explicit planning request plus task files

should_trigger.query: break this feature down into implementation tasks with acceptance criteria
create tasks for migrating our notifications to a provider-agnostic port
what's the plan for adding checkout, payment retries, and receipt emails?
sequence the catalog, cart, and checkout work into self-contained stories
prepare work items for the dashboard analytics feature
turn this product spec into tasks/issues with requirements and tests
scope the implementation for role-based project permissions
I need a sequenced plan before we start coding the billing feature
create an implementation backlog for user onboarding

should_not_trigger.query: review this pull request for auth bugs
how does the project invitation flow work today?
fix the typo in the settings page heading
debug why the checkout test fails
what are the pros and cons of PostgreSQL vs MongoDB?
summarize the API routes in this repository
add a dark mode toggle and run the tests
validate that the previous task was implemented correctly
make a quick prototype of the upload form without formal task structure
